mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 150263k in Dec 2018 showing sustained employment growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS trend versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 2.25 percent, the restrictive monetary stance raises short-term discount rates and credit costs, causing equity multiples to contract and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.27% in Dec 2018, above 2.25% threshold
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS > 2.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and an increase in the unemployment rate jointly signal contracting aggregate supply and demand, triggering earnings recession fears that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change negative while UNRATE 3-month change positive
failure_condition: In joint INDPRO-decline and UNRATE-rise episodes, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative less than 55% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sub-4-percent unemployment, accelerating industrial production signals an overheating supply side that invites monetary tightening and margin pressure, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% in Dec 2018 below 4% threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in advance retail sales indicates robust consumer demand that translates directly into revenue beats for consumer-facing corporations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 513531M in Nov 2018 indicating strong retail activity
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS growth versus 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes downward earnings revisions and risk-off flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% in Dec 2018 near historical lows
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated consumer price inflation and a restrictive federal funds rate compress real corporate margins and increase equity risk premiums simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both conditions hold.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 252.733 and FEDFUNDS at 2.27% in late 2018
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of joint high-inflation and high-rates episodes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend quarterly real GDP growth surprises reveal underlying demand momentum that drives positive earnings revisions, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18664.973B in Q3 2018 reflecting solid output
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 surprise versus 60-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
